summary: Advanced matching in the toy/trophy module, mainly src/melee/ty/toy.c, with small type-width fixes in gm_1601.c and a toy.h prototype cleanup. The decomp.dev GALE01 report recorded +528 matched bytes and four new 100% toy matches: un_80308354, un_803102D0, un_8030813C, and un_80309338. The same bot report also flagged remaining costs: un_80308250 broke from 100% to 98.57%, un_803075E8 regressed, and .sdata/.sdata2 percentages fell.

key_files.role: Main matching work: implemented un_803067BC, rewrote several toy/trophy helpers, normalized HSD assertions, adjusted LObj animation/list handling, and changed float/string/data references.
Header sync: changed un_8030813C arg0 from s16 to s32 and removed no-longer-used extern declarations for un_80308354 assert strings.
Small matching/type-width cleanup: changed local var_r3 from s16 to s32 in gm_801604DC and gm_80160564.

what_changed: Replaced the target stub for un_803067BC with C that copies s16 entries from un_804D6E64 into un_804D6EDC, using gmMainLib_8015CC90() for the count and separate forward/reverse-copy paths based on arg1.
Converted multiple toy.c OSReport + __assert pairs to HSD_ASSERTREPORT and added baselib/debug.h. Affected sites include un_80306D70, un_80307018, un_803075E8, un_8030813C, and un_80308354.

Improved un_80306EEC by using un_804DDCD8 for the 0.0f animation request, checking (*anims)->position_anim instead of next, and replacing direct prev->next assignment plus fake dead assert with HSD_LObjSetNext(prev, lobj).
Changed un_8030813C from s16 arg0 to s32 arg0 in both toy.c and toy.h, adjusted the model-table scan setup, and asserted with HSD_ASSERTREPORT when the toy model is not found.

Reworked un_80308354 to load target from un_804D6EDC[idx], scan the TrophyData table at un_804D6EC4 for a matching id, and use goto-shaped control flow plus HSD_ASSERTREPORT for the missing-model path. Bot reported this function improved from 68.06% to 100.00%.

Reworked un_80309338 distance/sqrt code by treating un_803B88D4 and constants un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, and un_804DDD90 as extern data and by spelling out the __frsqrte Newton iterations. Bot reported this reached 100.00%.

Reworked un_803102D0 to call lbArchive_LoadSymbols with string addresses derived from un_803FDD18 offsets instead of named str_TyDataf_dat/str_tyModelFileTbl/str_tyModelFileUsTbl labels. Bot reported this reached 100.00%.
Adjusted gm_801604DC and gm_80160564 locals from s16 to s32, likely to avoid unwanted halfword sign-extension/codegen differences.
smart_moves: Synchronized implementation and header when widening un_8030813C arg0 to s32, reducing the chance of caller/callee type drift during future matching.

Used real HSD helpers/macros where the original likely did: HSD_LObjSetNext replaced a direct field write and removed an explicit fake-match dead assert block, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT replaced several open-coded OSReport/__assert sequences.
Matched hard float code in un_80309338 by using external sdata2 constants and preserving the __frsqrte refinement structure rather than relying on decimal literals like 0.0, 0.5, and 3.0.
For un_803102D0, matched string-address codegen by indexing into the shared un_803FDD18 string blob, a useful tactic when named string labels produce different relocations or data ordering.
For un_803067BC, got an unmatched function to 82.64% by modeling the original pointer arithmetic and eight-entry unrolled reverse-copy loop before chasing a perfect match.
Cleaned toy.h by removing function-specific externs that became unnecessary after the assert rewrite, keeping local implementation details out of the public header.
decomp_lessons: Small s16 versus s32 choices matter: both gm_1601.c locals and the un_8030813C prototype changed only in width, but these choices affect sign-extension and caller codegen.

Assert/report reconstruction affects both code and data sections. HSD_ASSERTREPORT can be the right abstraction, but exact format strings and variadic arguments still need verification; the bot report showed un_803075E8 and data-section regressions after this PR.
Prefer HSD API helpers over open-coded struct writes when the helper encodes original side effects or inline assert behavior, as shown by HSD_LObjSetNext in un_80306EEC.
When matching math routines using __frsqrte, preserve volatile temporaries, operation order, and external constants; literal constants may not match the original sdata2 references.
String tables may need to be addressed through a shared base symbol plus offsets rather than through individually named string labels, as in un_803102D0 with un_803FDD18.

A callee match can break a nearby caller: decomp.dev reported un_8030813C reached 100%, but un_80308250 fell from 100% to 98.57%, so future work should check caller effects after prototype/code-shape changes.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions and data keep address-based names such as un_8030813C, un_804D6EDC, and un_803FDD18 until better domain names are proven.
toy.h keeps address comments on prototypes, e.g. /* 30813C */ for un_8030813C.
Ty-domain structs/types use Ty prefixes where known, including TyLightEntry, TyLightSymbol, and TyViewData; broader domain structs in this slice include ToyData and TrophyData.
Partially understood struct fields use x-offset names such as xFC_idx and xCC_name, while confirmed HSD fields such as position_anim should be used when known.
The corrected public signature for un_8030813C in this PR is char* un_8030813C(s32 arg0, enum_t unused).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Widen locals from s16 to s32 in gm_801604DC/gm_80160564 to shape integer codegen and avoid extra halfword operations.
Use s16 pointer arithmetic with char* byte offsets for un_804D6E64/un_804D6EDC copies in un_803067BC.
Use an eight-at-a-time unrolled reverse-copy loop controlled by count >> 3 and a remainder loop controlled by count & 7 in un_803067BC.
Use a deliberate empty no-op condition in un_8030813C, written as if ((arg0 && arg0) && arg0) {}, to influence code shape; reuse cautiously because it is a matching artifact.
Use register s32 target and explicit goto labels in un_80308354 to reproduce branch/return layout.
Declare un_803B88D4, un_804DDCD8, un_804DDD88, and un_804DDD90 as externs in the local C file so code references the same data symbols as the target.
Spell out __frsqrte Newton iterations with external constants in un_80309338 instead of compiler-friendly helper math.
Reference archive symbol names through un_803FDD18 + offsets in un_803102D0 to match string-pool addressing.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp-dev bot GALE01 report: matched code became 66.44% (+0.01%, +528 bytes). New 100% matches were un_80308354, un_803102D0, un_8030813C, and un_80309338.
decomp-dev bot also reported un_803067BC improved from 0.00% to 82.64% and un_80306EEC improved from 96.39% to 96.40%.
decomp-dev bot flagged regressions: un_80308250 broke from 100.00% to 98.57%, un_803075E8 dropped from 96.35% to 85.60%, and .sdata/.sdata2 percentages decreased.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching un_80308250; this PR's callee/prototype changes broke its 100% match to 98.57%.
Find later PRs touching un_803075E8 or toy.c assert strings; this PR's HSD_ASSERTREPORT rewrite coincided with an 85.60% regression there.
Search for later work that finishes un_803067BC beyond the 82.64% match introduced here.
Check whether .sdata and .sdata2 regressions from this PR were later recovered, especially around un_804DDCD8/un_804DDD88/un_804DDD90 and toy assert strings.
Search for PRs that name or refine ToyData, TrophyData, TyLightEntry, TyLightSymbol, and the model-file table structure used by un_8030813C/un_80308354.
Compare other lbArchive_LoadSymbols matches that use shared string blob offsets like un_803FDD18 + offset instead of individual str_* labels.
